docs.title: ⭐ Welcome to the StarNote Unofficial Wiki
docs.text:
Your comprehensive guide to mastering StarNote—the vector-based notebook designed for students and professionals. This community-maintained wiki provides a bridge between the app's current features and the community's future roadmap.

Important

This wiki was not made by the developers of StarNote and still a work in progress so it may not be completely accurate!

docs.title: 🚀 What is StarNote?
docs.text:
StarNote is a high-performance digital notebook that prioritizes a subscription-free experience without compromising on pro-level tools. Built on a proprietary ink engine, it offers:

  • Ultimate Infinite Canvas: Limitless vertical and horizontal scroll for expansive brainstorming and deep derivations.
  • Pro-Level PDF Toolkit: Advanced annotation, smart inversion for dark mode, and seamless page expansion.
  • Precision Creation: Low-latency handwriting, intelligent shape recognition, and recently added Smart Tables.
  • Privacy-First: Secure data with local storage and optional Google Drive/OneDrive backups.

docs.title: 🛠️ Getting Started
docs.text:
New to the app or the wiki? Start with these core sections:

  • Setup Guide: Optimize your stylus and cloud backup settings for the first time.
  • Feature Tracker: See the latest community-audited requests and bug reports.
  • FAQ: Quick answers to the most common user questions.

docs.title: Current Community Priorities:
docs.text:
1. LaTeX Support: Currently the #1 most requested feature for STEM students.
  2. Improved OCR & Handwriting Search: Tracking development updates for native text indexing.
  3. Cross-Platform Sync: Monitoring the rollout of advanced real-time synchronization.

docs.title: 💎 Support the Community
docs.text:
StarNote thrives on user feedback. If you have a suggestion or found a bug:

  1. Join the conversation on the StarNote Discord Server or the r/StarNoteApp subreddit.
  2. Wait for the devs or community members to respond.

docs.title: 🛠️ Quick Navigation
docs.text:
• Getting Started --- Set up your stylus and cloud backups. Setup Guide

  • Feature Tracker --- See what the community is requesting right now. Community Audit

  • Smart Tables --- Learn how to use the new v1.3.4 table tools. Table Guide

  • Common Questions --- Troubleshoot lag and export issues. FAQ

docs.title: 💾 Manual Backups & Data Archiving
docs.text: Because StarNote currently stores all data locally on your device, your notes are vulnerable if your device is lost, damaged, or the app is uninstalled. This guide covers the manual "fail-safe" methods to ensure your notes are never lost.

docs.title: 🛠️ The Manual Export Method (.starnote)
docs.text: The .starnote file is the app's native format. It preserves all vector data, layers, and "Smart Table" properties. Use this for full notebook backups.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Export
docs.text:
1. Select your Notebook In the main Library view, long-press on the notebook cover you wish to back up.
  2. Choose Export Tap the Export icon (usually a box with an upward arrow) and select StarNote Format (.starnote).
  3. Choose Destination Save the file to your device's internal "Files" app, or better yet, a physical USB-C drive or external SSD.

Best Practice

At the end of every quarter, we recommend exporting your entire module folder as a single .starnote archive.

docs.title: 📄 Exporting Notes
docs.text:
PDFs are Non-Editable

Once exported as a PDF, you cannot "re-import" the handwriting into StarNote as editable vectors.

  • Editable PDF: Best if you plan to annotate further in a different PDF reader.
  • Non Editable PDF: After being exported, cannot be further edited in other PDF applications, making it more suitable for file preservation.
  • Picture: Single exported image can be viewed directly, whereas for multiple selections, it must be unzipped before viewing.
  • StarNote: Full note contents retained in .starnote file format, backup or sharing supported.

docs.title: 🚨 Troubleshooting Corrupt Backups
docs.text:
If a backup fails to import, it is usually due to one of the following:

Issue Solution Insufficient Storage Ensure your tablet has at least 2GB of free space before attempting a large export. File Extension Error Ensure the file ends in .starnote. Do not manually rename the file extension. Version Mismatch A backup created in v1.3.4 may not open in v1.2.0. Always keep the app updated.

docs.title: Need to link to the Cloud?
docs.text: If you prefer an automated approach over manual files, head over to the Cloud Backup Guide to set up Google Drive or OneDrive integration.

docs.title: ❓ Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: This FAQ is compiled from common community discussions on Reddit and the official support channels. If you don't find your answer here, feel free to reach out on the StarNote Discord Server or the r/StarNoteApp subreddit.

docs.title: Is StarNote free to use?
docs.text: StarNote follows a "Freemium" model. Basic note-taking features and the infinite canvas are free. Advanced features like OCR (Handwriting-to-Text), specific premium templates, and advanced cloud sync options may require a one-time purchase or subscription.

docs.title: Which platforms is StarNote available on?
docs.text: Currently, StarNote is optimized for iOS (iPad) and Android (Tablets). A Windows/Desktop version is one of the most highly requested features—see our Feature Tracker for updates on its status.

docs.title: Does StarNote support the Apple Pencil / S-Pen?
docs.text: Yes. The app is specifically designed for low-latency input with active styluses. It supports pressure sensitivity and palm rejection.

docs.title: How do I use the Infinite Canvas?
docs.text: The canvas expands automatically as you write near the edges. Use a two-finger drag to pan around your workspace and a pinch gesture to zoom in or out.

docs.title: How do I export my notes as a PDF?
docs.text:
1. Open the note you wish to export.
  2. Tap the Three dots icon in the top right corner.
  3. Select Export.
  4. Choose a format and whether you want to export and share or just export.

docs.title: Where are my notes stored?
docs.text: By default, notes are stored locally on your device. If you enable Cloud Backup (OneDrive or Google Drive), a copy is encrypted and uploaded to your chosen provider.

docs.title: Can I sync my notes to my Windows PC?
docs.text: Not yet. Because there is no native Windows app, you can only view your notes on PC by exporting them as PDFs and uploading them to your cloud drive or transferring them manually.

docs.title: Can I sync my notes to my other devices?
docs.text: StarNote does not currently support live syncing. If you edit a note on your device, it will not automatically appear on your other devices in real-time. You must manually "Restore" from a backup to move data between devices. While Real-Time Live Syncing (device-to-device instant updates) is not yet a feature of StarNote, you can manually transfer your data using the built-in Cloud Backup system.

docs.title: How often should I backup?
docs.text: The more often the better. We recommend using the Auto-Backup setting.

docs.title: What happens if I delete the app?
docs.text: If you haven't set up a Cloud Backup or exported your files manually, your notes will be permanently lost. Always ensure your backup screen stays open until the status says "Success" before closing the app.

docs.title: Can I share a pro account between an iPad and an Android tablet?
docs.text: Yes! Simply log in with the same account and sign in method on both devices to sync your pro status. No need to pay more than once. Click here to see how to backup or move notes between devices.

docs.title: My handwriting feels laggy. How can I fix this?
docs.text:
Lag is usually caused by high background CPU usage. Try the following:

  • Close other resource-heavy apps.
  • Check if "Low Power Mode" is enabled on your device, as this limits screen refresh rates.
  • Submit a bug report from inside the app if the issue is still not resolved.

docs.title: When will my features be added?
docs.text:
Ultimately, the developers will say when features will release, but you can see the current request volume on the Most Requested Features page.

Tip:

If your question isn't answered here, check the Setup Guide for initial configuration tips or visit the StarNote Discord Server or the r/StarNoteApp subreddit for real-time help.

docs.title: 🚀 Feature Tracker
docs.text:
# 🛰️ StarNote Community Suggestions (April 2026)

## ⚙️ STEM Needs
* **LaTeX Math Support**
    * Inline rendering
    * Equation to Text
* **Integrated Tools**
    * Scientific Calculator
    * Unit Converter
    * Function Plotter

## ✏️ Workflow & Lasso
* **Persistent Lasso Auto-Scroll** (Edge detection)
* **Space-Maker Tool** (Select All Below)
* **Object Grouping**
* **Precise Scaling** (Numerical input)
* **Custom One Stroke Shaping Delay**
* **More custom gestures**
* **Full Keyboard Support** (With Shortcuts and mouse/trackpad input)
* **Stylus Tilt Detection** (e.g. for pencil like in Sketchbook app)
* **Option to Export Selection** (Especially for infinite note type)
* **Word-like note mode** (For text editing in markdown/latex, with inkboxes for drawing)

## 📂 Platforms & Sync
* **Native Windows App**
* **Incremental Live Sync**
* **WebDAV Integration**
* **Multiple Starnote App Instances for advanced multitasking**

## 🎨 Tool Improvements
* **Scribble to Erase** (for iOS)
* **Pressure Curve Tuner**
* **Infinite Color Palettes**
* **Audio Markers** (Timestamps)

## 📐 Shape Intelligence
* **Node Editing**
    * Drag-and-drop anchor points
    * Curve manipulation (Bézier handles)
    * Snap to grid options
    * Custom grids (isometric, 1, 2, 3 point perspectives, cartesian, polar, etc.)
    * Relative shape alignment
    * Equidistant snapping
    * Smart Color Fill
* **Technical Shapes**
    * Square Root symbol
    * Coordinate Axis generator
    * Logic Gate and Circuit Diagram library (STEM)
* **Metadata Overlay**
    * Permanent Angle labels
    * Real-time Side Lengths

## 📲 Navigation & UI
* **Sticker Categories** (Folders)
* **Image Backround Removal for Sticker Creation**
* **Smart Reminders** (Sticker alerts)
* **Page-Specific Themes**
* **Custom Toolbar Anchoring**
* **Themes & Aesthetics**
    * **True AMOLED Theme**
    * **Custom UI Theme Colors** (Background Color, Background Image, Buttons, sidebars, etc.)
    * **Community Theme Marketplace**
    * **Stormy/Rainy Backgrounds** (Live background)

docs.title: Index old
docs.text:
Important

This wiki was not made by the developers of StarNote and still a work in progrees so it may not be completely accurate!

docs.title: 🌟 StarNote Unofficial Wiki
docs.text: Welcome to the comprehensive unofficial feature guide for StarNote, a high-performance note-taking application designed for students and professionals. This wiki reflects features as of v1.3.4 (April 2026).

docs.title: 🖋️ Writing & Input Engine
docs.text:
• Variable Pen Tools:
    • Fountain Pen: Pressure-sensitive for calligraphy and expressive writing.
    • Ballpoint: Uniform line width, ideal for technical diagrams.
    • Highlighter: Transparent ink that layers without obscuring underlying text.
  • SoftPaint Brush (v1.2.0+): A specialized brush for soft-edge shading and artistic sketching.
  • Canvas Options:
    • Infinite Canvas: Limitless scrolling in all directions—ideal for brainstorming or large flowcharts.
    • Fixed-Size Pages: Standard templates including A4, Letter, and Legal.
  • Smart Shapes: A "Hold-to-Snap" gesture that automatically converts rough strokes into perfect geometric shapes (circles, triangles, rectangles, etc.).
  • Precision Control: Dedicated Palm Rejection and Pen-Only Mode to prevent accidental touches from interfering with your stylus work.

docs.title: 📊 Organization & Data Management
docs.text:
• Native Tables (v1.3.4): Insert structured tables with adjustable rows and columns. Supports cell background coloring and direct handwriting within cells.
  • Advanced Layering: Manage multiple transparent layers per page. Layers can be locked, hidden, or adjusted for opacity—perfect for complex engineering overlays.
  • Sticker & Element Library: Save frequently used symbols, diagrams, or math snippets as "Stickers" for one-tap insertion.
  • Hierarchical Filing: Organize notebooks into a system of Nested Folders (e.g., Engineering > Year 3 > Electrotechnique).
  • Outline View: Generate a clickable Table of Contents within long documents for rapid navigation.

docs.title: 🎨 Visuals & Customization
docs.text:
• Smart Inversion (Dark Mode): A global dark mode that intelligently inverts paper and ink colors while preserving the original colors of inserted images and stickers.
  • Custom Paper Templates: Import any PDF or PNG to serve as a recurring page background (useful for specialized engineering grids).
  • Dynamic UI:
    • Moveable Toolbar: Dock the tool palette to the top, bottom, or sides.
    • Floating Palette: A compact, movable tool menu to maximize writing space.
  • Custom Color Palettes: Supports HEX code input and a color dropper tool for precise aesthetic control.

docs.title: 📂 PDF & Document Handling
docs.text:
• High-Fidelity Rendering: Maintains sharpness at extreme zoom levels, essential for reading small-print data sheets or blueprints.
  • Active Annotations: Robust engine for importing large PDF textbooks and adding annotations.
  • Hyperlink Support: Full navigation for internal document links and external web URLs.
  • Side-by-Side View: Open two notes, or a PDF and a note, simultaneously in a split-screen layout.
  • Flexible Exports: Export as "Flattened PDFs" (permanent annotations) or "Editable PDFs" (layered objects).

docs.title: 🔐 Privacy, Sync & Security
docs.text:
• Local-First Philosophy: Primary data is stored on-device for maximum speed and offline reliability.
  • Google Drive Integration: Semi-automatic backup and restore functionality to safeguard data across devices.
  • Biometric Security: Secure specific notebooks or the entire app via fingerprint or passcode.
  • One-Time Purchase: A lifetime license model that avoids recurring subscription fees.

docs.title: 🛠️ Roadmap & Community Requests
docs.text:
• LaTeX Support: Currently a top-requested feature for students to convert handwriting into digital formulas.
  • Handwriting Search (OCR): While typed text is searchable, searching handwritten ink is in active development.
  • Real-Time Auto-Sync: Developers are currently working on a seamless "Edit on Tablet, View on Phone" sync capability.

docs.title: 🏆 Most Wanted Features
docs.text:
Rank Feature Request Frequency Est. Upvotes Priority Status 1 LaTeX / Math Formula Support High 450+ 🔴 Critical 2 OCR & Handwriting Search High 380+ 🔴 High Demand 3 Native Windows / Desktop App Medium 310+ 🟠 Strategic 4 Object Grouping Medium 240+ 🟠 Quality of Life 5 Scrollable Color Palettes High 120+ 🟢 UI Polish 6 Real-Time Cross-Platform Sync Medium 95+ 🟠 High Demand

Results aggregated using Google Gemini

docs.title: 📤 PDF Exporting & Sharing
docs.text: Converting your infinite canvas notes into a standard PDF is usually the final step in the StarNote workflow. This page covers how to convert your expansive notes into a format that anyone can view on any device.

docs.title: 🛠️ The Export Workflow
docs.text:
StarNote treats the PDF export as a "snapshot" of your vector data.

Vector Fidelity

Because StarNote uses a vector engine, your exported PDFs will remain crisp and searchable (if you used typed text).

docs.title: Step-by-Step Guide
docs.text:
1. Open the Export Menu Tap the Export File icon (square with upward arrow) either in the toolbar or under the three dot menu.
  2. Choose PDF Type Choose between Editable and Non-Editable (Flattened) PDF Types.
  3. Choose a File Name
  4. Choose whether or not to include the page background
  5. Choose between export only or export and share
Please Note

StarNote always exports a local copy to your device (storage/documents/starnote/export) for both export only and export and share.

docs.title: 📋 PDF Export Options
docs.text:
• Flattened PDF --- Merges all layers into a single image. Best for submissions where you don't want your handwriting to be accidentally moved by the recipient.

  • Editable PDF --- Preserves layers so that other PDF annotators can see your strokes as separate objects.

docs.title: ⚠️ Important Considerations
docs.text:
Handwriting Searchability

As of v1.3.4, StarNote does not embed OCR (Optical Character Recognition) data into exported PDFs. While your handwriting looks crisp, you cannot "Ctrl+F" your handwritten notes within a PDF viewer yet.

  • File Size: Large infinite canvases with many high-res image imports can result in PDFs exceeding 50MB.
  • Hyperlinks: Any internal links created within StarNote are currently non-functional in exported PDFs. They will appear as standard text.

docs.title: Need to save the original file?
docs.text: If you want to move your work to another tablet while keeping it fully editable, visit the Manual Backups (.starnote) page instead.

docs.title: 🖋️ Pens & Writing Tools
docs.text: StarNote’s proprietary vector ink engine ensures that every stroke remains crisp at any zoom level. Unlike raster-based apps, your text and diagrams will never pixelate, regardless of how far you zoom into the infinite canvas.

docs.title: 🖌️ The Five Core Pens
docs.text:
Select a pen type to see its specific characteristics and best-use cases for your studies.

Fountain PenCalligraphy PenSoftbrush PenBallpoint PenPencil
  • Best For: Expressive headings and personal signatures.
  • Behavior: Highly responsive to pressure(adjustable); mimics the flow of a real nib.
  • Note: Width is determined solely by downward pressure.
  • Best For: Titles and stylized labels.
  • Behavior: Uses a "chiseled" nib logic where line thickness changes based on the direction of your stroke.
  • Note: Excellent for making module covers look professional.
  • Best For: General sketching and bold emphasis.
  • Behavior: A softer, more organic edge compared to the technical pens.
  • Note: Provides a consistent "inky" feel.
  • Best For: Technical derivations and active note-taking.
  • Behavior: The most stable and predictable pen. It maintains a consistent width regardless of speed or pressure.
  • Best For: Drafting, brainstorming, and light annotations.
  • Behavior: Mimics graphite texture.
  • Note: Perfect for initial layouts before "inking" over them with the Ballpoint or Fountain pen.

docs.title: ⚙️ Writing Settings & Optimization
docs.text:
• Shape Recognition --- Draw any basic shape and hold at the end of the stroke. StarNote will snap the line into a perfect geometric vector.

  • :material-some-stroke:{ .lg .middle } Pressure Sensitivity --- Adjust the sensitivity curve in Settings > Stylus. (Applicable to Fountain Pen and Pencil).

  • Palm Rejection --- Toggle Stylus Only Mode in the top toolbar to prevent accidental "ghost strokes" from your hand.

  • Stroke Stabilization --- The engine automatically smooths handwriting jitters to keep your notes looking clean at high zoom levels.

docs.title: 🛠️ Getting Started with StarNote
docs.text: Welcome to the community! This guide will walk you through setting up StarNote for the first time, from installation to optimizing your workflow.

docs.title: 1. Installation
docs.text:
StarNote is currently available for mobile and tablet devices. Use the links below to ensure you are downloading the official version:

  • iOS / iPadOS: Download on the App Store
  • Android: Download on Google Play or use the direct apk download on their website.

docs.title: 2. Initial Configuration
docs.text: When you first open the app, we recommend configuring these three core settings to ensure your data is safe and your experience is smooth.

docs.title: Cloud Backup
docs.text:
By default, notes are stored locally on your device. To access them across devices or ensure they are backed up:

  1. Go to Settings > Cloud backup.
  2. Select and bind your preferred provider (OneDrive or Google Drive).
  3. Toggle Automatic backup to ON.

Importnant!

Cloud backup is different from live syncing, which is not yet implemented. Live syncing is still in development and is expected to launch late Q2.

docs.title: Stylus & Pencil Optimization
docs.text:
If you are using an Apple Pencil, S-Pen, or third-party stylus, calibrate it for the best handwriting experience:

  • Pressure Sensitivity: Adjust the pressure sensitivity in your pen settings to match your writing style.
  • Palm Rejection: Ensure "Palm Rejection" is enabled in gesture settings if you write with your hand resting on the screen.

docs.title: Default Templates
docs.text:
Save time by setting your favorite paper style as the default for all new quick notes:

  • Navigate to Settings > Document settings > Quick note default template.
  • Choose from the built in or custom templates.

docs.title: 3. Core Features Overview
docs.text:
To get the most out of the app's infinite canvas, keep these basic gestures in mind:

Action Gesture Pan / Scroll Drag with two fingers Zoom In/Out Pinch with two fingers Erase Use the eraser tool Select / Lasso Draw a circle around elements using the lasso tool to move or resize them

docs.title: 4. Staying Updated
docs.text: Since StarNote is evolving rapidly based on community feedback, check the Feature Tracker to see what new tools are currently being developed or requested by users like you.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
If you run into issues during setup:

  • Check our FAQ for common troubleshooting steps.
  • Visit the StarNote Discord Server or the r/StarNoteApp subreddit to ask the community for advice.

docs.title: ☁️ Cloud Backup & Data Safety
docs.text:
Ensuring your notes are backed up is critical for any user. While Real-Time Live Syncing (device-to-device instant updates) is not yet a feature of StarNote, you can protect your data using the built-in Cloud Backup system.

Live Sync vs. Cloud Backup

StarNote does not currently support live syncing. If you edit a note on your device, it will not automatically appear on your other devices in real-time. You must manually "Restore" from a backup to move data between devices.

docs.title: 🛠️ Setting Up Automated Backups
docs.text:
StarNote allows you to link your favorite cloud provider to store snapshots of your .starnote library.

Google DriveOneDrive
  1. Open StarNote and tap the Settings (gear icon) in the top-left.
  2. Navigate to Cloud backup
  3. Select bind Google Drive and sign in with your student or personal account.
  4. Toggle Auto-Backup to ON.

Storage Tip

Since StarNote uses vector data, backups are typically very small (usually under 5MB per notebook). Even a free Google Drive account could last you through your entire degree.

  1. Open StarNote and tap the Settings (gear icon) in the top-left.
  2. Navigate to Cloud backup
  3. Select bind OneDrive and sign in with your student or personal account.
  4. Toggle Auto-Backup to ON.

Storage Tip

Since StarNote uses vector data, backups are typically very small (usually under 5MB per notebook). Even a free OneDrive account could last you through your entire degree.

docs.title: 📂 Moving Data Between Devices
docs.text:
If you are switching from one device to another, follow this "Snapshot" method:

  1. On the Original Device: Go to Cloud Backup and tap Upload Backup.
  2. On the New Device: Sign in to the same cloud provider and account.

  3. Restore: Tap Download & Restore.

    Warning

    This will create duplicates any existing notes on the new device in addition to the versions from the cloud. If you want to avoid duplicates and you know what you are doing, you can select and delete all of your notes before restoring a backup to simply have a single copy of the backed up notes. Note that any changes that are not backed up will be lost in this way! Backups cannot be merged. If you want to keep a note that's not in the backup being restored, simply do not delete it.

docs.title: 💾 Local Backups
docs.text:
If you prefer not to use the cloud, or if you want to archive a specific module's notes at the end of the semester, use the Local Export feature.

Local BackupImporting a Backup
  1. Open StarNote and tap the Settings (gear icon) in the top-left.
  2. Navigate to Local backup
  3. Tap "Backup now"
  4. Optionally toggle Auto-Backup to ON.
  1. Open StarNote and tap the Settings (gear icon) in the top-left.
  2. Navigate to Local backup
  3. Tap "Import" and select the backup file you would like to import.

docs.title: Where are my notes stored?
docs.text: By default, notes are stored locally on your device. If you enable Cloud Backup (OneDrive or Google Drive), a copy is encrypted and uploaded to your chosen provider.

docs.title: Can I sync my notes to my Windows PC?
docs.text: Not yet. Because there is no native Windows app, you can only view your notes on PC by exporting them as PDFs and uploading them to your cloud drive or transferring them manually.

docs.title: Can I sync my notes to my other devices?
docs.text: StarNote does not currently support live syncing. If you edit a note on your device, it will not automatically appear on your other devices in real-time. You must manually "Restore" from a backup to move data between devices. While Real-Time Live Syncing (device-to-device instant updates) is not yet a feature of StarNote, you can manually transfer your data using the built-in Cloud Backup system.

docs.title: How often should I backup?
docs.text: The more often the better. We recommend using the Auto-Backup setting.

docs.title: What happens if I delete the app?
docs.text: If you haven't set up a Cloud Backup or exported your files manually, your notes will be permanently lost. Always ensure your backup screen stays open until the status says "Success" before closing the app.

docs.title: Can I share a pro account between an iPad and an Android tablet?
docs.text: Yes! Simply log in with the same account and sign in method on both devices to sync your pro status. No need to pay more than once. Click here to see how to backup or move notes between devices.

docs.title: Tools
docs.text:
Important

This wiki was not made by the developers of StarNote and may not be completely accurate!

docs.title: Tools
docs.text:
This guide covers the technical specifications and customization options for pens.

Remember

Some of these features will only work properly when using an active stylus.

docs.title: Core Pen Types
docs.text: StarNote uses a "Pen Box" system allowing for 16 custom presets. Tap any tool in the toolbar twice to access its specific configuration menu.

docs.title: 1. Fountain Pen
docs.text:
• Behavior: A reliable pen that works well for writing naturally.
  • Dynamics: Always has some level of pressure sensitivity, even when set to 0%.
  • Settings:
    • Pressure Sensitivity
    • Thickness
    • Stroke Stabilization

docs.title: 2. Calligraphy Pen
docs.text:
• Behavior: A fancy pen that works well for making beautiful headings or labels.
  • Dynamics: Makes thicker lines on downwards strokes and thinner lines on upwards strokes.
  • Settings:
    • Thickness
    • Stroke Stabilization

docs.title: 3. SoftPaint Brush (v1.2+)
docs.text:
• Behavior: A flowy pen that that's great for sketching or making headings.
  • Dynamics: Fills the stroke as you write and tapers the end based on the writing speed.
  • Settings:
    • Thickness

docs.title: 4. Ballpoint Pen
docs.text:
• Behavior: A consistent pen great for making precise diagrams or neat handwriting and drawing.
  • Dynamics: Uniform line width regardless of pressure or speed.
  • Settings:
    • Thickness
    • Stroke Stabilization

docs.title: 5. Pencil
docs.text:
• Behavior: A semi transparent pen great for coloring, shading or making quick sketches.
  • Dynamics: Appends brushes to existing strokes, making them darker until saturated.
  • Settings:
    • Pressure Senstivity
    • Thickness
    • Density

docs.title: Pen Settings
docs.text:
Customize how the strokes react to your stylus to match your writing style.

  • Pressure Sensitivity - Makes the pen stroke thickness (and density in the case of the pencil) react to the writing pressure measured by your tablet's stylus. Force-thickness curves are not fully customizable however.
  • Thickness - Adjust the thickness of the stroke in millimeters.
  • Stroke Stabilization - Reduces "jitters" caused by writing and enables neater handwriting and better curved strokes.
  • Density - Changes the hardness level of the pencil so it writes darker or lighter.
  • One-Stroke Shaping - If you hold the stylus at the end of a stroke, the app snaps the line into a perfect version of the shape drawn. See the shapes tool for more info on supported shapes for this tool.

docs.title: Utility Writing Tools
docs.text: Tools designed for interaction and correction.

docs.title: Highlighter:
docs.text:
• Behavior: Semi-transparent ink.
  • Dynamics: Layers behind existing handwriting to prevent obscuring notes.

docs.title: Other Highlighter Options:
docs.text: • Draw Straght Line: Automatically snaps your highlight to a straight line.

docs.title: Eraser:
docs.text: A tool to precisely remove anything you want.

docs.title: Eraser Modes:
docs.text:
• Stroke Eraser: Deletes the entire continuous line/shape.
  • Partial Eraser: Deletes only the pixels the eraser touches (standard eraser).
  • Circular selection eraser: Deletes everything within a region you circle around.

docs.title: Other Eraser Options:
docs.text:
• Size: Changes the size of the eraser brush.

  • Scribble to Erase: Enables erasing content by simply scribbling it out. (1)

    1. Only available on android.

  • Erase Highlighter Only: Makes so the eraser tool only removes highlighter without disturbing other elements.

  • Erase Tape Only: Makes so the eraser tool only removes tape without disturbing other elements.
  • Select Stroke:
  • Auto-Deselect Eraser: When enabled, the app automatically returns back to the tool you used before, once you lift the stylus after erasing.

docs.title: Lasso:
docs.text: Tool for selecting objects in your notes in order to move or edit them.

docs.title: Lasso Modes:
docs.text:
• Free Lasso: Makes so you can draw a shape exactly around what you want to select.
  • Rectangular Lasso: Makes use of a rectangular selection box.

docs.title: Other Lasso Options (Include or Exclude):
docs.text:
• Shape
  • Picture/Sticker
  • Tape
  • Text box
  • Handwriting
  • Highlighter
  • Link
  • Tag
  • Table

docs.title: Picture Tool:
docs.text: Allows the user to insert images directly into their notes.

docs.title: Picture Tool Options:
docs.text:
• Insert From Album: Allows the user to insert an image from the device gallery.
  • Take Photo: Opens the camera to take a picture to insert.

docs.title: Textbox:
docs.text: Allows the user to insert typed text directly into their notes.

docs.title: Textbox Options:
docs.text:
• Font Type: Changes the face of the font.
  • Font Size: Changes the width and height of the font.
  • Multiply: Unknown
  • Font Styles:
    • Bold
    • Italics
    • Underline
  • Alignment:
    • Left
    • Center
    • Right
  • Font Color: Features a set of 5 preset, but customizable colors.

docs.title: Excerpt Pen:
docs.text: This is a tool for "citing" parts of your notes either within the same note, or to another note, allowing you to jump back to the original note. (Jumping not available when placing the excerpt in the same note)

docs.title: Excerpt Pen Modes:
docs.text:
• Text Excerpt: Allows exceprting typed text in your notes.
  • Box Excerpt: Allows excerpting any part of your notes.

docs.title: Excerpt Pen Options:
docs.text: • Show the excerpt icon: Enables or disables the icon on excerpts.

docs.title: Tagging Tool:
docs.text: Allows the user to add new or existing tags to their notes. These tags are resizable and can be filtered by in the home screen side bar.

docs.title: Shape Tool:
docs.text: Allows the user to add specialized shapes to their notes.

docs.title: List of the Available Shapes:
docs.text:
• Straight Line
  • Straight Wave Line
  • Arc
  • Straight Dashed line
  • Straight Arrow
  • Straight Double Sided Arrow
  • Curved Arrow
  • Curved Double Sided Arrow
  • Angled Lines
  • 2D Axes
  • 3D Axes
  • 2D Axes with 45º arrow
  • Curly Bracket
  • Scalene Triangle
  • Isoceles Traingle
  • Equilateral Triangle
  • Right Triangle
  • Square
  • Rectangle
  • Rounded Rectangle
  • Pill Shape
  • Scalene Trapezoid
  • Isoceles Trapezoid
  • Right Trapezoid
  • Rhomboid
  • Diamond
  • Rhombus
  • Pentagon
  • Hexagon
  • Octagon
  • Circle
  • Ellipse
  • Star
  • Cone
  • Triangular Pyramid
  • Square Pyramid
  • Various Triangular Prisms
  • Rectangular Prism
  • Cube
  • Pentagonal Prism
  • Hexagonal Prism
  • Cylinder
  • Tube
  • Truncated Rectangular Pyramid
  • Truncated Cone
  • Ellipsoid
  • Sphere
  • Half Sphere

docs.title: Shape Tool Options:
docs.text:
• Stroke Thickness: Changes the width of the lines used for drawing the shapes.
  • Shape Fill: Allows for a color to be selected as the fill color of the shape. Can also be synced with the stroke color of the shape, with a specified opacity.

docs.title: Sticker:
docs.text: • Allows users to place one of the preexisting or custom added stickers to their notes, primarily for decoration.

docs.title: The Tape Tool:
docs.text: Creates opaque, removable strips. Tap to "reveal" what is underneath. * Usage: Perfect for Active Recall—hide formulas or answers while studying.

docs.title: Tape Tool Modes:
docs.text:
• Draw Straight Line: Makes the tape tool only draw in straight lines.
  • Rectangle Drawing: Makes the tape tool able to draw rectangular shapes at custom sizes.

docs.title: Tape Tool Options:
docs.text:
• Show All Tapes: Toggle to show or hide all tapes in the note.
  • Style: Changes the pattern displayed on the tape surface.
  • Thickness: Changes the width of the tape for the straight tape mode.
  • Clear tape on current page: Removes all tape drawn on the current page of the note.
  • Clear tapes on all pages: Removes all tape drawn anywhere in the current note.

docs.title: The Laser Pointer:
docs.text: A temporary ink tool for "drawing" attention to parts of a diagram during a presentation or review session. The ink disappears after a few seconds.

docs.title: Laser Pointer Modes:
docs.text:
• Line: Leaves a trail when moving the laser pointer.
  • Dot: Only shows the laser pointer dot.

docs.title: Other Laser Pointer Options:
docs.text:
• Thickness: Changes the size of the pointer.
  • Duration: Changes the length of time the laser stays before fading away.

docs.title: Ruler:
docs.text: Toggle to show a ruler on the screen that you can move or rotate using two fingers. The measurement units are not specified, but angle is in degrees. When drawing towards or against the ruler, the line snaps against the straight edge and stays there until the end of the stroke.

docs.title: Magnifier:
docs.text: Tap to show a magnified view of the zoom box.

docs.title: Magnifier Usage:
docs.text:
• The zoom box is resizable, but the magnified view is not.
  • The magnifier is designed particularly for writing purposes, and the arrow buttons are designed to help with it. Simply hit the left or right arrow buttons to move the zoom box in that direction. Hit the "enter" arrow to move the box downwards to the next line and back to the left of the page. When auto-advance is toggled on, the zoom box moves automatically when your stroke crosses the resizable edge area on the right.
  • To close the magnifier, hit the cross on the magnified view.

docs.title: Table:
docs.text: The table tool allows users to insert tables into their notes.

docs.title: Table Tool Modes:
docs.text:
• Header Row: Toggles whether the drawn table has a header row or not.
  • Header Column: Toggles whether the drawn table has a header column or not.
  • Rows: Changes the number of rows in the drawn table. (1-50)
  • Columns: Changes the number of columns in the drawn table. (1-50)
  • Border Thickness: Changes the thickness of the table borders.
  • Fill Color: Toggles whether the table cells have a fill color and gives options to change the color when enabled.

docs.title: Color & Aesthetic Control
docs.text:
• Hex Code Support: Input specific 6-digit codes for exact color matching.
  • Color Dropper: Select any color from an imported PDF, image, or screenshot to add to your custom palette.
  • Transparency Slider: Adjust the "Alpha" channel for any pen type to create custom layered effects.
